page.content: The first table presents diagnostic criteria for hereditary cancer syndromes, with headers including Criteria, Description, and Reference. The Classic criterion requires a proband diagnosed with a STS before age 45, one first-degree relative with any tumor before age 45, and another first- or second-degree relative diagnosed with any cancer before age 45 or a STS at any age, as per Li and Fraumeni, 1988.

lines.text: The first table presents diagnostic criteria for hereditary cancer syndromes, with headers including Criteria, Description, and
Reference. The Classic criterion requires a proband diagnosed with a STS before age 45, one first-degree relative with any
tumor before age 45, and another first- or second-degree relative diagnosed with any cancer before age 45 or a STS at any age,

page.html:
CriteriaDescriptionReference
Classic• Proband diagnosed with a STS before age45 AND;- One first-degree relative with any tumor diagnosed before age45 AND;- Another first- or second-degree relative diagnosed with anycancer before age 45 or a STS at any ageLi and Fraumeni, 1988
Birch• Proband with a cancer diagnosed inchildhood (STS, CNS, ADR) OR;• Proband diagnosed with a STS, SNC, orADR before age 45 AND;- One first- or second-degree relative diagnosedwith a tumor from the LFS spectrum at any age AND;- First- orsecond-degree relative diagnosed with any cancer before age 60Birch, 1994
Eeles• LFL-Eeles 1- Two first-or second- degree relatives diagnosed with a typical LFStumor at any age• LFL-Eeles 2- Proband diagnosed with a STS at any age AND;- Two first-orsecond-degree relatives diagnosed with two different typical LFS tumors at anyageEeles, 1995
Chompret• Proband diagnosed with a typical LFStumor before age 36 AND;- One first- or second-degree relativediagnosed with any cancer before age 46 OR;- One relative with multipletumors diagnosed at any age• Proband with multiple primary tumors -including two typical LFS tumors - with the first diagnosed before age 36regardless of family history• Proband with ADR at any age regardless offamily historyChompret, 2001
Chompret 2009• Proband with a typical LFS tumordiagnosed before age 46 AND;- At least one first- or second-degreerelative diagnosed with a typical LFS tumor (except breast cancer if theproband is/was affected by breast cancer) before age 56 or with multipleprimary tumors OR;• Proband with multiple primary tumors (other thanmultiple breast tumors) - including at least two from LFS tumor spectrum - withthe first diagnosed before age 46;• Proband diagnosed with ADR or CPCat any age irrespective of family historyTinat, 2009

page.content: The Birch criterion involves a proband with childhood cancer (STS, CNS, ADR) or diagnosed with a STS, SNC, or ADR before age 45, one first- or second-degree relative with a tumor from the LFS spectrum at any age, and another relative diagnosed with any cancer before age 60, according to Birch, 1994. The Eeles criterion specifies LFL-Eeles 1 with two first- or second-degree relatives diagnosed with a typical LFS tumor at any age and LFL-Eeles 2 requiring a proband diagnosed with a STS at any age and two first- or second-degree relatives with different typical LFS tumors at any age, as stated by Eeles, 1995. The Chompret criterion involves a proband diagnosed with a typical LFS tumor before age 36 and related conditions, as established in Chompret, 2001, and later expanded in Chompret 2009. The second table details antibody characteristics with headers Antibody, Source, Non-neoplastic mucosa, and Altered expression in cancer, specifying for instance, that Cytokeratin, sourced from mouse monoclonal clone AE1/AE3, has a cytoplasmic expression and exhibits loss in cancer. Other antibodies listed include E-cadherin, TTF-1, β-catenin, Vimentin, Snail, Twist, and CD44, each with specific sources and expressions detailed accordingly.

lines.text: The Birch criterion involves a proband with childhood cancer (STS, CNS, ADR) or diagnosed with a STS, SNC, or ADR before
age 45, one first- or second-degree relative with a tumor from the LFS spectrum at any age, and another relative diagnosed with
any cancer before age 60, according to Birch, 1994. The Eeles criterion specifies LFL-Eeles 1 with two first- or second-degree
relatives diagnosed with a typical LFS tumor at any age and LFL-Eeles 2 requiring a proband diagnosed with a STS at any age
and two first- or second-degree relatives with different typical LFS tumors at any age, as stated by Eeles, 1995. The Chompret
criterion involves a proband diagnosed with a typical LFS tumor before age 36 and related conditions, as established in
